changes.old: was regarded by Garibaldi at that time as shrewd, yet foreshadowed by Dante
changes.new: was regarded by Garibaldi at that time as an end, yet foreshadowed by Dante
changes.reason: 'come fine' = the noun 'il fine' (an end/goal), not the adjective 'fine' (shrewd) — settled by the parallel two paragraphs later: 'non come fine, ma come mezzo' (not as an end, but as a means). Restores the end/means motif. Refuted-finding re-check overturn.
